controls.title: Network Security Controls
controls.description: Network security controls are implemented to protect the cardholder data environment from unauthorized access. Firewall and router configurations restrict inbound and outbound traffic to only that which is necessary and deny all other traffic.
evidence: Firewall ruleset documentation showing default-deny posture with justification for each permitted rule
Network diagram illustrating CDE segmentation boundaries and all connections into and out of the cardholder data environment
Firewall configuration review records demonstrating at least semi-annual ruleset review and approval
Network access control list configurations for routers and switches protecting CDE segments

sub_controls.title: Define and understand all components in scope
Network security controls (NSCs) are configured and maintained
Network access to and from the cardholder data environment is restricted
Network connections between trusted and untrusted networks are controlled
Risks to the CDE from computing devices able to connect to both untrusted networks and the CDE are mitigated

controls.title: Secure Configurations
controls.description: System components are protected from known vulnerabilities by developing configuration standards aligned to industry-accepted hardening guidelines. Default vendor-supplied credentials are changed and unnecessary default accounts are removed prior to deployment.
evidence: Hardening standard documentation referencing industry benchmarks such as CIS or NIST for each system type in scope
Configuration compliance scan results showing system components measured against approved baselines
Configuration management database or inventory confirming vendor defaults have been changed for all in-scope systems
Change records demonstrating hardening standards are applied prior to production deployment

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for applying secure configurations are defined and understood
System components are configured and managed securely
Wireless environments are configured and managed securely

controls.title: Protect Stored Account Data
controls.description: Storage of sensitive authentication data is minimized or eliminated, and primary account numbers are protected wherever stored. Strong cryptography is used to render stored cardholder data unreadable when storage is required.
evidence: Data flow diagram identifying all locations where primary account numbers and sensitive authentication data are stored
Encryption configuration or key management documentation for databases and storage systems containing cardholder data
Data discovery scan results demonstrating no unencrypted PAN storage outside approved and documented repositories
Data classification policy and data retention schedule with records of disposal of data exceeding retention periods

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for protecting stored account data are defined and understood
Storage of account data is kept to a minimum
Sensitive authentication data (SAD) is not retained after authorization
Access to displays of full PAN and ability to copy PAN are restricted
Primary account number (PAN) is secured wherever it is stored
Cryptographic keys used to protect stored account data are secured
Where cryptography is used to protect stored account data, key management processes and procedures are implemented

controls.title: Protect Data in Transit
controls.description: Strong cryptography is used to safeguard primary account numbers during transmission over open, public networks. Trusted keys and certificates are used, and insecure protocols are disabled to prevent interception of cardholder data in transit.
evidence: TLS configuration reports or SSL scan results confirming use of current strong cryptography and absence of insecure protocols
Inventory of all transmission paths carrying cardholder data across open or public networks with encryption confirmation
Certificate management records showing valid, unexpired certificates and trusted certificate authority usage
Network capture or protocol analyzer evidence confirming cardholder data is not transmitted in cleartext

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for protecting cardholder data with strong cryptography during transmission are defined and understood
PAN is protected with strong cryptography during transmission

controls.title: Protect Against Malicious Software
controls.description: Anti-malware solutions are deployed on all systems commonly affected by malicious software to protect those systems from current and evolving malware threats. Anti-malware mechanisms are maintained current, actively running, and generating audit logs.
evidence: Anti-malware deployment report showing coverage across all in-scope systems with active protection status
Malware definition or signature update logs confirming frequent and current updates across all endpoints
Endpoint protection scan logs and quarantine records demonstrating active detection and response capability
Anti-malware exception register with documented business justification and compensating controls for any excluded systems

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for protecting all systems and networks from malicious software are defined and understood
Malicious software (malware) is prevented, or detected and addressed
Anti-malware mechanisms and processes are active, maintained, and monitored
Anti-phishing mechanisms protect users against phishing attacks

controls.title: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems
controls.description: Security vulnerabilities in system components are identified and protected through security patches and a formal software development lifecycle that incorporates security requirements. Bespoke and custom software is developed in accordance with secure coding guidelines.
evidence: Patch management policy and patching SLA records showing critical patches applied within defined timeframes
Static application security testing scan results integrated into the CI/CD pipeline for in-scope applications
Secure software development policy referencing OWASP or equivalent standards with developer training records
Vulnerability and patch status report showing all in-scope system components are within approved patch levels

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for developing and maintaining secure systems and software are defined and understood
Bespoke and custom software are developed securely
Security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed
Public-facing web applications are protected against attacks
Changes to all system components are managed securely

controls.title: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
controls.description: Access to system components and cardholder data is limited to only those individuals whose job requires such access, following a need-to-know and least-privilege principle. Access control systems are in place to restrict access to system components.
evidence: Access control policy defining need-to-know and least-privilege requirements for cardholder data environments
User entitlement review records showing periodic recertification of access rights to in-scope systems
Role-based access control configuration showing separation of duties and appropriate access restrictions
Access provisioning workflow records with documented business justification and approval for CDE access grants

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for restricting access to system components and cardholder data by business need to know are defined and understood
Access to system components and data is appropriately defined and assigned
Access to system components and data is managed via an access control system(s)

controls.title: Identify Users and Authenticate Access
controls.description: All users are assigned a unique ID before allowing them to access system components or cardholder data, and authentication is managed through strong authentication mechanisms. Multi-factor authentication is required for all access into the cardholder data environment.
evidence: MFA enrollment and enforcement reports demonstrating all CDE access requires multi-factor authentication
IAM platform configuration showing unique user ID requirements, shared account prohibitions, and password policy enforcement
Authentication logs demonstrating individual accountability and absence of shared or group credentials in the CDE
Account management records showing timely disabling of terminated user accounts within defined SLA

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for identifying users and authenticating access to system components are defined and understood
User identification and related accounts are strictly managed throughout an account's lifecycle
Strong authentication for users and administrators is established and managed
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is implemented to secure access into the CDE
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) systems are configured to prevent misuse
Use of application and system accounts and associated authentication factors is strictly managed

controls.title: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data
controls.description: Physical access to systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data is controlled and restricted to authorized individuals. Physical access controls are documented and enforced through procedures for granting, monitoring, and revoking physical access.
evidence: Physical access control system logs for data centers and CDE areas showing authorized entry records and anomaly review
Visitor log records demonstrating escort procedures and logging of all non-employee physical access
Physical security policy covering access authorization, badge issuance, and periodic access list review
Media handling procedures and destruction records for physical media containing cardholder data

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for restricting physical access to cardholder data are defined and understood
Physical access controls manage entry into facilities and systems containing cardholder data
Physical access for personnel and visitors is authorized and managed
Media with cardholder data is securely stored, accessed, distributed, and destroyed
Point-of-interaction (POI) devices are protected from tampering and unauthorized substitution

controls.title: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
controls.description: Audit logs are implemented to track and monitor all access to system components and cardholder data, enabling the reconstruction of events and detection of anomalous activity. Logs are protected from destruction and unauthorized modifications and reviewed at least daily.
evidence: Log management policy defining which systems generate logs, required log fields, retention period of at least twelve months, and online availability requirements
SIEM configuration showing ingestion of logs from all in-scope CDE systems with correlation rules and alerting
Evidence of daily log review including SIEM alert records, analyst review artifacts, or automated review reports
Log integrity controls such as write-once storage, cryptographic hashing, or centralized forwarding preventing tampering

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for logging and monitoring all access to system components and cardholder data are defined and documented
Audit logs are implemented to support the detection of anomalies and suspicious activity
Audit logs are protected from destruction and unauthorized modifications
Audit logs are reviewed to identify anomalies or suspicious activity
Audit log history is retained and available for analysis
Time-synchronization mechanisms support consistent time settings across all systems
Failures of critical security controls are detected, reported, and responded to promptly

controls.title: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
controls.description: Security vulnerabilities are discovered and remediated through regular vulnerability scanning and penetration testing of all in-scope system components and network segments. Intrusion detection mechanisms are deployed to detect and alert on intrusions into the network.
evidence: Quarterly internal and external vulnerability scan reports with evidence of passing scans or remediation of high and critical findings
Annual penetration test report from qualified internal resource or approved scanning vendor covering CDE scope
Remediation tracking records showing vulnerability findings addressed within defined risk-based timeframes
Intrusion detection or prevention system configuration and alert logs demonstrating active monitoring of CDE traffic

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for regularly testing security of systems and networks are defined and understood
Wireless access points are identified and monitored, and unauthorized wireless access points are addressed
External and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritized, and addressed
External and internal penetration testing is regularly performed, and exploitable vulnerabilities and security weaknesses are corrected
Network intrusions and unexpected file changes are detected and responded to
Unauthorized changes on payment pages are detected and responded to

controls.title: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs
controls.description: An information security policy that addresses all PCI DSS requirements is established, published, maintained, and disseminated to all relevant personnel. A security awareness program is implemented to make all personnel aware of the importance of cardholder data security.
evidence: Information security policy document with evidence of annual review, management approval, and distribution to all personnel
Security awareness training completion records demonstrating all personnel trained upon hire and at least annually
Third-party and vendor risk assessment records including due diligence documentation and PCI DSS responsibility matrices
Incident response plan with evidence of annual testing and defined roles, responsibilities, and escalation procedures

sub_controls.title: A comprehensive information security policy governing the protection of the entity's cardholder data and CDE is known and current
Acceptable use policies for end-user technologies are defined and implemented
Risks to the cardholder data environment are formally identified, evaluated, and managed
PCI DSS compliance is managed
PCI DSS scope is documented and validated
Security awareness education is ongoing
Personnel are screened to reduce risks from insider threats
Risks associated with third-party service providers (TPSPs) are managed
Third-party service providers (TPSPs) support their customers' PCI DSS compliance
Suspected and confirmed security incidents that could impact the CDE are responded to immediately
